facts.primaryTitle: The Killer Was Already Inside the House
facts.setting: Elena believed the dog beneath her bed was calming her.
criticalEvents: Elena believed the dog beneath her bed was calming her.
By morning, the dog was dead in the hallway—and someone had written a message on the mirror.
Elena Ward was a university student house-sitting for her aunt when the story began in a rain- soaked suburban house on the edge of Brighton.
The first reports did not describe an obvious monster or violent attack.
facts.threat: Elena believed the dog beneath her bed was calming her.
facts.finalConsequence: The final warning is therefore simple: when the same impossible detail appears twice, do not wait for a third occurrence to prove that it is real.
facts.unresolvedQuestion: Did Elena Ward defeat the thing at the centre of the story, or did the encounter simply teach it how to remain hidden?
